channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Wall Street Journal Editorial Board Questions Whether Trump's Iran Agreement Guarantees Reopening of the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A recent editorial by the Wall Street Journal's editorial board expressed concerns about a memorandum of understanding (MOU) reached between President Donald Trump's administration and Iran regarding ...

item.title: Sen. Roger Marshall Draws Criticism After Echoing Trump's Defense of Iran Retaining Missiles
item.description: Sen. Roger Marshall (R-Kansas) faced significant criticism on social media after repeating a statement similar to one made by former President Donald Trump regarding Iran's military capabilities. Duri...

item.title: Former Trump aide criticizes Iran deal and predicts denial response from Trump
item.description: A former senior member of Donald Trump’s communications team, Anthony Scaramucci, has publicly criticized the former president following the announcement of a recent Iran-related agreement. Speaking o...

item.title: Commentators Draw Connections Between Peter Thiel's Dialog Network and Names Appearing in Epstein-Related Records
item.description: A Raw Story article discusses reactions to a recent Wired report that published a list of more than 200 members associated with Dialog, a private network organized by technology investor Peter Thiel. ...

item.title: Nintendo offers a free bonus month for Switch Online + Expansion Pack 12-month memberships in the US
item.description: Nintendo is offering a limited-time promotion for users of the Switch Online + Expansion Pack service in the United States. Players who purchase or redeem a 12-month individual or family membership fo...

item.title: Ariane 6 Debuts More Powerful P160C Boosters on Amazon Leo Satellite Mission
item.description: Arianespace successfully launched an Ariane 64 rocket from the Guiana Space Centre on the VA269 mission, marking the first flight of the Ariane 6 equipped with new P160C solid rocket boosters. The mis...

item.title: Study Finds Elevated Stress Accumulation at Southern California's Cajon Pass Fault Junction
item.description: A recent study published in the Journal of Geophysical Research has identified unusually high levels of accumulated tectonic stress at Cajon Pass, a critical fault junction in Southern California wher...

item.title: Equatorial Guinea government resigns after missing performance targets
item.description: The government of Equatorial Guinea has collectively resigned following what officials described as a significant failure to meet governing objectives. Prime Minister Manuel Osa Nsue Nsua formally sub...

item.title: Trump Pledges Georgia Campaign Rallies for Mike Collins as Critics Question Their Electoral Impact
item.description: President Donald Trump celebrated Rep. Mike Collins' victory in the Republican nomination race for the U.S. Senate seat in Georgia and announced plans to actively campaign on Collins' behalf. In a soc...

item.title: The 1965 Griffith Series 200 Combined a Lightweight British Chassis with a Powerful Ford V8
item.description: The article highlights the 1965 Griffith Series 200, a rare Anglo-American sports car that successfully combined British engineering principles with American high-performance power. Built by hand in S...

item.title: Ted Cruz and Donald Trump Jr. Clash Over Iran Policy Remarks and MAGA Loyalty
item.description: A public dispute has emerged between U.S. Senator Ted Cruz (R-TX) and Donald Trump Jr., highlighting tensions within conservative and MAGA-aligned political circles. The controversy began after Cruz, ...

item.title: Producers United Adds Top Entertainment and Labor Attorneys to Advance Producer Rights Initiatives
item.description: Producers United, an advocacy organization representing approximately 300 film and television producers, has expanded its legal and labor expertise by bringing on renowned entertainment litigator Bonn...

item.title: Socioeconomic Factors Behind Female Criminality in Early 20th-Century New York City
item.description: This article examines the realities of female criminality in New York City during the early twentieth century, challenging the sensationalized portrayals commonly found in newspapers of the era. While...

item.title: Portland Chefs, Bar, and Writer Earn Three James Beard Awards in 2026
item.description: Portland received significant recognition at the 36th annual James Beard Awards held in Chicago, with local culinary and literary figures winning three prestigious honors. Ryan Roadhouse, chef and own...

item.title: Rare Behind-the-Scenes Photos Highlight the Making and Legacy of The Panic in Needle Park
item.description: Vintage Everyday published a collection of photographs featuring Al Pacino and Kitty Winn during the production of the 1971 film “The Panic in Needle Park.” The article revisits the importance of the ...

item.description: Former Department of Homeland Security Chief of Staff Miles Taylor argued that the United States has lost significant credibility among many of its traditional allies due to President Donald Trump's f...

item.title: Mark Levin Criticizes Trump’s Iran Agreement and Its Treatment of Hezbollah
item.description: Fox News host Mark Levin publicly criticized President Donald Trump’s newly announced Memorandum of Understanding with Iran, marking a significant disagreement between the conservative commentator and...

item.title: NASA ends MAVEN mission after spacecraft failure, concluding key Mars atmospheric loss research
item.description: The NASA MAVEN mission to Mars has officially ended after more than a decade of operations, following the loss of contact with the spacecraft on December 6, 2025. Although attempts were made to reesta...

item.title: Oklahoma Republican Candidate Jackson Lahmeyer Plans to Withdraw After Controversy Over Messages With Former Fundraiser

item.title: Alexis Wilkins Scheduled to Perform at U.S.-Sponsored Freedom 250 Event in Brussels
